During a meeting, officials conveyed India's efforts to safeguard its diaspora and energy needs, while Opposition demanded parliamentary debate.

articles.title: Trump reacts as Hegseth says US will keep pressure on Iran

raw.title: Trump reacts as Hegseth says US will keep pressure on Iran
raw.description: This is how Donald Trump reacted to Pete Hegseth stating that the US will keep on applying pressure in Iran. Speaking in the Oval Office on Tuesday (24 March), the Secretary of Defense said that it was his job to ensure that the US “keeps our hand on that throttle” and continues attacks for “as long and as hard as necessary”. The president could be seen raising his eyebrow at the remark. His comment appears to be in contrast to Mr Trump’s 15-point plan, which is aimed at ending the war for good. Elsewhere in the conference, Mr Trump said that Mr Hegseth does not want to end the conflict. “Our Secretary of War doesn't want peace, he wants war,” he said.

This is how Donald Trump reacted to Pete Hegseth stating that the US will keep on applying pressure in Iran.

articles.title: Iran Dismisses US Ceasefire Plan, Issues Counterproposal As Strikes Land Across Mideast

raw.title: Iran Dismisses US Ceasefire Plan, Issues Counterproposal As Strikes Land Across Mideast
raw.description: Dubai, Mar 25: Iranian state television’s English-language broadcaster quoted an anonymous official on Wednesday saying that Iran had rejected America’s 15-point ceasefire proposal. Pakistani officials described the proposal broadly as touching on sanctions relief, civilian nuclear cooperation, a rollback of Iran’s nuclear program, monitoring by the International Atomic Energy Agency, missile limits and access for shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf. The Trump administration reportedly offered the plan to Iran as the U.S. […]
